trivia_bullets.text: Part of the Mirage diamond cycle (Mirage, 1996): five uncommon artifacts each costing {2}, entering tapped, tapping to produce one aligned-color mana. Fire Diamond produces {R}. This Commander Legends (CMR) printing released November 20, 2020.
sources: MTGSalvation forums: Diamond cycle thread, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Artist Lindsey Look confirmed in a 2018 Hipsters of the Coast interview that she used her dad's hand as the model for Fire Diamond — the pale hand cradling the glowing red gemstone in the art. Her grandmother's hand appears in Charcoal Diamond; her mom's hand in Lion's Eye Diamond.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: 'Lindsey Look's Powerstone Shard,' 2018

trivia_bullets.text: Original Mirage printing (1996) illustrated by Richard Thomas. Look repainted all five diamonds for Commander 2014 (2014-11-07); every Fire Diamond printing from C14 forward uses her art. Commander Legends: Battle for Baldur's Gate (2022) also features two alternate-art reprints by different artists (Gaboleps, Phil Stone).

"The essence of flame to stoke the spirit." Parallel construction to Charcoal Diamond's "The essence of night to harden the soul" — identical syntactical pattern, elemental essence + verb + effect on bearer's inner state.
EDHREC rank 669 — budget red mana accelerant for Commander. Most common in mono-red or heavy-red decks with budget constraints. Legal in Legacy, Pauper, Commander, Pauper Commander.

Artist-prized signal: Lindsey Look's Talisman cycle originals sold combined $15,625 (2019), Gilded Goose sold $7,211 (2019), Sublime Epiphany won Chesley Award. Diamond cycle paintings belong to the same body of work from one of Commander's most collected illustrators.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: 'Modern Horizons Talisman Cycle by Lindsay Look Sold,' 2019
Hipsters of the Coast: 'Lindsey Look's Gilded Goose Sells for $7,211,' 2019
